titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the C. K. Williams Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday March 12, 2021 at 04:18

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Writings, correspondence, notebooks, journals, teaching materials and other papers by, to, or relating to poet C. K. Williams.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into seven series: I. Writings, 1950s-2011. II. Correspondence, 1950s-2011. III. Writings of Others, 1980s-2010. IV. Photographs and Personal Papers, 1966, 1980s. V. Printed Materials, 1971-1996. VI. Audiovisual Materials, 1970s-1980s. VII. Electronic Media, circa 2000-2007.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 111-112 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 113 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The C. K. Williams Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

bioghist.head: C. K. Williams
bioghist.p: C. K. Williams (1936-2015) was an American poet and translator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from James S. Jaffe Rare Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2012.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes poetry and prose typescripts and manuscripts, with related correspondence.

did.unittitle: Small notebooks and miscellaneous writings with correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes folders with the following title: King; "The Poet;" various; Claire; Zipes; Translations from Anthologie Personnelle; Rage; Bits and Pieces and Essays; Heine, Adam; essays for book; notes to August '69; notes Oct. '63 - Apr. 66 to about '79 - '80.

did.unittitle: Drafts of poems and other writings including "In the Forest," "The Night Santa Clause Cried," and "The Past," as well as business correspondence
Printed materials, notebooks, typescript for "Common Knowledge, galley proof, and other papers
Early reviews of first books and essays, other reviews, and essays with related correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes comments on "Repair" by Alicia Ostriker.

did.unittitle: Incomplete manuscripts of "Lies" and "I Am Me Bitter Name"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains drafts and rejected poems from "I Am Me Bitter Name".

did.unittitle: Incomplete manuscripts of "Lies" and "I Am Me Bitter Name"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains drafts and rejected poems from "I Am Me Bitter Name".

did.unittitle: Drafts: "Further Noted to Dionysus Monologue" and "The Bacchae of Euripides"
Pocket notebooks and drafts relating to Flesh & Blood
Drafts and proofs, with some faxed correspondence
Drafts: Film, The Operated Jew, The Vigil.
Flesh and Blood (French proof), Criminals, The Situation of Poetry in America
Page proofs, The Bacchae of Euripides: A New Version by C. K. Williams, with corrections
Drafts and proofs, including Flesh and Blood.
Drafts: "She Through," "Dream of Mind," Paul Zweigs "Selected," Essay on Tragedy
Drafts early version of "Dream of Mind," drafts labelled "A Little Loneliness," and other drafts
Miscellaneous drafts, poems, essays, and traslations
Drafts: "Creatures of Love," early version of "Bacchae," and "Vigil"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains writings by others including Alan Shapiro, Ted Weiss, Renee Weiss, Stephen Dunn, Marina Shron, Louise Glück, James Richardson, Laurie Scheck, and Robert Pinsky.

did.unittitle: Corrected drafts, including: Repair and The Operated Jew.
"On Whitman" drafts, Berlin drafts, and orrespondence with Ellen Foos

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes wwritings by Jane Hirshfield, Clair Malroux, and Barbara Ras.

did.unittitle: Drafts including "War," Conversations with CK Williams and Alan Fox, "Letter to a German Friend," "The Operated Jew," and others

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes drafts by other writers including Isabelle Miller, Peter Campion, Paul Muldoon, Louise Glück, Daisy Fried, and Jim Quinn; and business correspondence.

did.unittitle: Drafts including "On Whitman," Wait, and others
"Collected Poems, 1963-2006" proofs, master pages, first pass, final pass, setting pass, and other proof types
Various drafts, including material from "The Operated Jew," "The Singing"
Drafts and notes relating to essays, book chapters, and other writings
Correspondence, including friends, family, and business

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes manuscripts from other writers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes manuscripts from other writers.

did.unittitle: Correspondence with publishers; news clippings and photocopies of reviews

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents includes Tex Williams, Jessie Williams, Claire Malroux, Walter Thümler, Jed Williams, Efe Murat Balikcioglu and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes materials related to the Teachers and Writers Collabrative.

did.unittitle: Correspondence from collegues dealing with publishing, academics and writing; postcards; greeting cards, and other correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains photocopies and clippings.

did.unittitle: Correspondence, photographs from an event at Columbia High School, mail art
Letters, emails and faxes to and from collegues and friends with news clippings and photocopies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by Jim Moore, Bill Tremblay, Mark Craver, Stephen Berg, Michael Storper, Alan Shapiro, Paul Zweig, Ruth Hammond, Bill P. Root, Agnes Lynch Starrett, William Heyen, S. J. Marks, and R. Goodman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by Robert Pinsky, Richard Cole, Galway Kinnel, Roger Lewis, William Pitt Root, Peter Klappert, and Steve Kasdin. Also includes correspondence and course materials.

did.unittitle: Writings of others, correspondence, and related printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by Robert Pinsky, Stephen Berg, Alan Shapiro, Galway Kinnell, S.J. Marks, Tzvetan Todorov, Debra Nystrom, Mark Strand, Adam Zagajewski, Stuart Dischell, Jerrold Freedmand, Martha Nussbaum, John Deane, Chuck Guilford, Ruth Padel, L.S. Asekoff, and Philip Levine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by L.S. Asekoff, Tzvetan Todorov, Mark Rudman, Stephen Berg, David, Roskies, Alan Shapiro, Galway Kinnell, Jonathan Galassi, Carrie Allen McCray, Robert Pinsky, Lauren Mullen, Paul Muldoon, Tom Sleigh, Mark Craver, Jack Zipes, Chase Twitchell, Nancy, Huston, E.C. Hinsey, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by Mark O'brien, Fredrick Seidel, Tess Gallagher, S.J. Marks, Michael Hofmann, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains drafts, including: "The Emerging Figure," by S. BenTov, "Riffs and Reciprocities," by Stephen Dunn, "On Men Weeping," by Alan Shapiro, and "In Quest for the Genuine Storyteller," by Jack Zipes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes drafts by Chase Twichell, Edward Hirsch, Tom Sleigh, David Abram Milanaik, Emily Mann, and Alan Shapiro.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by Alane Rollings, Elisabeth Minawski, James Richardson, Emily Lundin, Paul Muldoon, Carolyn Forche, Russell Banks, Mark Chaver, Robert Pinsky, Ana Maria Shua, L.S. Asekoff, Tom Sleigh, Sandra Kohler, T.S. Flanagan, Chris Abani, Matthew Arnold, and Lea Weems.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by Jack Zipes, Theodore Weiss, Lindsay Knowlton, and Jake Lamar.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by David Havird, Dan Chiasson, Russell Banks, Steve Orlen, and Harold Schimmel. Also includes notes and a calandar.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by David Roskies, Alicia Ostriker, David Bonanno, F.C. Swain, Peter Chelnik, Leigh Jackson, Peter Clark, Sarah Maguire, Craig Arnold, Troy Jollimore Emily Mann, Noah Haidle, Pawel Marcinkiewicz, Alan Shapiro, Leon Wieseltier, Anthony Minghella, K.A. Perrymann, Galway Kinnell, and Martin Harries. Also includes dead matter from "Repair."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by Michael Donaghy, Jonathan Galassi, Paul Muldoon, Denise Lanctot, and others

did.unittitle: Poems by Madeline Carr, Gabrielle Levin, Jane Shore, and others

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by Paul Muldoon, David Groff, Craig Arnold, Eugene Motale, Richard Martin, Gary Adelmann, Fernando Pessoa, Katie Johntz, and Ted Weiss.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by Alan Shapiro, Fred Wander, Alice Cone, Susan Hahn, Lindsey Knowlton, Elise Paschen, Jeffery Skinner, Jack Klaff, Alicia Ostriker, Robert Pinsky, Mike Blumenthal and others. Also includes a translation by Edmund Keeley.

did.unittitle: Proofs of Hardheaded Weather by Cornelius Eady

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by E. Pages, R. Pinsky, J. Cone, P. Muldoon, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by Robert Pinsky, Alan Shapiro, Stephen Dobyns, Jeffery Shotts, Jeff Dolven, Bruce Weigl, Bill Whelan, Paul Muldoon, Giamco Leopardi, Chenxin Jiang, Rachel Galvin, Michael Grabell, and Don Thompson.

did.unittitle: Negatives, contact sheets, photographs, address books, and other personal papers
Catalogs, magazines, catalogs, event programs, and writings
Wheatland Foundation, American Poet Conference Material, and other printed material
Catalogs, clippings, ephemera, magazines, and other printed materials

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
